name: Q6210962
abstract: José Estevez Jr. (March 26, 1921 – March 7, 1988), professionally known as Joe Loco, was a Latin jazz and Latin pop pianist and arranger of Puerto Rican ancestry who was born in New York City.

about_quotes.speaker: Mario Bauza
about_quotes.evidence: I had two, three piano players, Louie Varona, Gilberto [Frank] Ayala, Bellonás, Robert Alila, Joe Loco.

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Loco who was the pianist at that time with the Machito band was drafted, and Mario Bowser who was their musical director just drafted me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: I met Joe. I say, 'Do you wanna do some one nighters for me?' ... I can book you in Newark. I can book you in the Palace... The Palladium.
